name: Hoomaluhia Botanical Garden
tagline: 400 acres of lush gardens backed by the Koolau cliffs — free admission and the most photogenic spot on Oahu.
parking: Free parking at visitor center and multiple lots throughout the garden
hours: Daily 9am–4pm. Closed Christmas & New Year's Day
bestTime: Early morning for misty mountain views and cooler temps; weekdays to avoid weekend crowds
talkStory: Built by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers in 1982 as a flood-control project, this 400-acre garden doubles as a giant retention basin protecting Kaneohe from Koolau runoff -- most visitors have no idea they are walking through an engineering marvel. The 32-acre lake offers free catch-and-release fishing with loaner poles on weekends. The Philippines and Sri Lanka botanical sections are the least visited but most lush.
